intent: Write a 3-source research memo on 'nutrition-conscious grocery choices for weight management on a $75 weekly budget'. (1) One Stop Market shopping site — browse the grocery-gourmet-food category and collect at least 5 products relevant to protein, fiber, healthy fats, and whole grains. Record name, price, rating, review count. (2) Reddit — browse /f/personalfinance AND /f/science for posts on budget grocery hacks, macro-tracking, or skepticism of marketing claims. Collect at least 3 posts with title, score, comment count. (3) Wikipedia — open 'Dietary fiber' and 'Protein (nutrient)' (or equivalent canonical pages) and extract 3-4 facts about recommended intake. The report must: (a) design a $75 weekly cart from the storefront with running totals and per-serving macros where inferrable, (b) back each selection with a Wikipedia citation on the relevant nutrient, (c) inject community tips on grocery-hack efficiency, (d) close with three rules for grocery-store decision-making. Cite every claim.
author_notes: Hard 3-site. Grocery + budget + nutrition science. Weekly cart with macro reasoning.
